sections.paragraph:
fut. יִפְחַד-

(1) to tremble-(a) for fear, to fear, Deu 28:66 Isa 12:2 followed by מִן Psal. 27:1 119:161 Job 23:15, and מִפְּנֵי Isai. 19:16 , of the pers. or thing feared. פָּחַד פַּחַד to fear a fear, Job 3:25. Used in a pregnant sense, פָּחַד אֶל־רֵעֵהוּ to turn with fear to one another, Jer 36:16 compare Gen 42:28.-(b) with joy, Isa 60:5 Jer 33:9.-The notion of fear is transferred to shame in the noun פַּחַד No. 2.

(2) to be in trepidation, i.q. to hasten, Hos 3:5. Compare נִבְהַל, נֶחְפַּז, חָרַד.

Piel, i.q. Kal, but intensitively, to fear continually, to be timid, followed by מִפְּנֵי Isa 51:13 to take care of oneself, to be cautious, Pro 28:14. (Opp. to הִקְשָׁה לֵב)

Hiphil, to cause to fear, to terrify, with an acc. Job 4:14. Hence פַּחְדָּה and-
